articles.title: Website is served from a 200KB binary

articles.title: Why study Diophantine equations?
articles.summary: Diophantine equations are fundamental in number theory and have led to the discovery of many hidden structures in integers. Studying these equations leads directly to concepts such as divisibility and remainder, which are crucial in modular arithmetic. By analyzing Diophantine equations like \(Ax = B\) or \(Ax + By = C\), one can gain insights into unique prime factorization and other mathematical ideas.

articles.title: Unauthenticated RCE in Motorola's MR2600 Router
articles.summary: Motorola's MR2600 router allows for unauthenticated Remote Code Execution through a two-step process involving uploading malicious firmware and triggering a validation-and-flash routine. The flaw lies in the way the SEAMA image is validated, which incorrectly checks the first four bytes of the file instead of extracting the relevant field from the form. This vulnerability can be exploited by claiming to send a multipart form but actually sending the raw firmware image without any boundary, bypassing the authentication check that would normally reject such requests.

articles.title: In defense of not understanding your codebase
articles.summary: In defense of not understanding your codebase, many developers assume that complete knowledge is necessary for good work, but this view overlooks the complexities of large software systems and the limitations of partial understanding. A key finding in Peter Naur's paper Programming as Theory Building suggests that a programmer's "theory of the program" can be partially captured by their intuitive sense of what's happening and why, which can only be partially expressed through code or documentation. This perspective is particularly relevant for large systems with high turnover rates where teams may experience significant changes in personnel over time.

articles.title: Good Tools Are Invisible
articles.summary: A good tool should strive to make itself invisible, not just by hiding its shortcomings but also by making them seem like a fun puzzle to solve. This is particularly true when it comes to text editors like vim, which can be frustratingly limited in their ability to handle certain tasks. When you're proficient with your editor of choice and it lacks the tools you need, it becomes invisible, but treating that friction as the "fun" part can lead people to advertise it as evidence of its greatness. In contrast, a good tool should focus on making itself useful without drawing attention to its limitations.

articles.title: An update on the scraper situation
articles.summary: The scraper situation continues to grow, with residential and mobile networks directing central command-and-control nodes to attack web sites. These nodes are often installed on ordinary systems that fetch web pages on demand and forward the data back to a controller. This traffic is difficult to block because it comes from multiple IP addresses simultaneously, making it challenging to identify and shut down. The problem has worsened in recent months, with media-streaming devices being identified as sources of scraper activity.
